subject: biology
title: 22M.1.SL.TZ1.6
question_html:
What are linked by hydrogen bonds?

A. Hydrogen and oxygen within a water molecule

B. Phosphate and sugar within a DNA molecule

C. Base and sugar between DNA nucleotides

D. Hydrogen and oxygen in different water molecules
examiners_html: Only 40 % of candidates answered this question correctly. A common misunderstanding was to think that hydrogen bonds are within water molecules (intramolecular) rather than between water molecules (intermolecular).
